title: Weird output when multiple podman commands are pasted into a terminal
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

> Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:
>
/kind bug
>
> /kind feature

**Description**
When you paste, for example, three podman commands in a row into a terminal, something strange happens with the output. Honesty, I can't quite tell what is happening. It feels like some kind of strange race condition.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Paste these commands in:

podman run fedora:24 python3 --version
podman run fedora:25 python3 --version
podman run fedora:26 python3 --version


2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

This is what podman does :-) The first command outputs after the last one is pasted in.

[root@fedora ~]# podman run fedora:24 python3 --version
podman run fedora:25 python3 --version
podman run fedora:26 python3 --version
Python 3.5.1


**Describe the results you expected:**

Like Docker:
[root@fedora ~]# docker run fedora:24 python3 --version
Python 3.5.1
[root@fedora ~]# docker run fedora:25 python3 --version
Python 3.5.4
[root@fedora ~]# docker run fedora:26 python3 --version
Python 3.6.2

Podman should do this:
[root@fedora ~]# podman run fedora:24 python3 --version
Python 3.5.1
[root@fedora ~]# podman run fedora:25 python3 --version
Python 3.5.4
[root@fedora ~]# podman run fedora:26 python3 --version
Python 3.6.2

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 0.2.2
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora 27 VM running on RHEL7 CSB Workstation (don't make fun of me, sleep mode, batter life, and docking station work flawlessly) :-)

comments.body:
...Huh.
This is a properly strange one. I wonder if it has something to do with how we handle attaching?

Can confirm. More trivial example:
```
podman run fedora:24 echo hi
echo bye
```

Looks like someone is/something is reading and flushing stdin.

so what about this:
```
± |master → origin ↑1030 ?:42 ✗| → rpm -q docker
docker-1.13.1-44.git584d391.fc27.x86_64

 2018-02-28 20:08:16 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/cri-o
± |master → origin ↑1030 ?:42 ✗| → sudo docker run -it fedora:24 python3 --version
sudo docker run -it fedora:25 python3 --version
sudo docker run -it fedora:26 python3 --version
Python 3.5.1

```

Ok the replicator is even simpler:

```
± |master → origin ↑1030 ?:42 ✗| → docker run alpine echo hi
hi

 2018-02-28 20:12:05 ⌚  localhost in ~/git/cri-o
± |master → origin ↑1030 ?:42 ✗| → echo bye
bye
```

but if you add the -i in, then the interactive console must absorb it and it doesn't work.  if I recall correct, podman run with -d is equiv to -it.  I suppose we could remove that assumption but God only knows what the impact might be.
@baude We can probably remove the -i, no reason to force interactive if we are. But we can't attach without a terminal right now - conmon limitation.
Ok, i looked into this more ... it was -t that was being set automatically if ! -d.  Disabling that did nothing for this problem.  There must be something more subtle going on.
I can't seem to replicate this anymore. Possibly the attach cleanups fixed it?
Closing, @fatherlinux Reopen if you can still reproduce.
